key_insight: The paper turns residual-based PINN outputs into deterministic, pointwise error intervals by reconstructing a compatible finite-element field and certifying its residual through a numerical system.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical way to trust PINN predictions in solid mechanics and other PDE applications by bounding local solution error rather than relying only on sampled loss values.
method: Reconstruct an admissible network prediction as a mesh-based compatible field.
Apply the finite-dimensional system to get a certifying residual and compute exact or approximate Green/adjoint error representations.
Use norm-based, localized, randomized, and comparison-estimate variants to produce computable pointwise intervals, including nonlinear extensions via Taylor remainders.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on manufactured examples, a large-scale 3D elasticity benchmark, and projected neural load families, with calibration and compatibility assessed.
watch_item: The strongest guarantees depend on mesh compatibility and on having a computable transfer from the discrete target to the continuous solution, which may be costly or problem-specific in practice.
paper_title: Pointwise Error Estimates for Numerical Physics-Informed Neural Networks
